en.extract:
Laura's woodland warbler (Phylloscopus laurae) is a species of Old World warbler in the family Phylloscopidae.
It is found in Angola, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Tanzania, and Zambia. It is named in honour of Laura Boulton, an American ethnomusicologist.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forests and subtropical or tropical swamps.

'''Laura's woodland warbler''' ('''''Phylloscopus laurae''''') is a species of [[Old World warbler]] in the family [[Phylloscopidae]].
It is found in [[Angola]], [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]], [[Tanzania]], and [[Zambia]]. It is named in honour of [[Laura Boulton]], an American [[Ethnomusicology|ethnomusicologist]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Mrs Boulton and the woodland warbler |url=https://blogs.bl.uk/sound-and-vision/2018/07/mrs-boulton-and-the-woodland-warbler.html |access-date=2025-02-08 |website=blogs.bl.uk |language=en}}</ref>

Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical dry [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical [[swamp]]s.
